title: Anatomy of the Normal Distribution
description: A visual guide depicting the key characteristics and properties of the normal (Gaussian) distribution, a fundamental concept in statistical modeling.
elements: Horizontal Axis (x) - Represents the data values
Vertical Axis (y) - Represents the probability density
Bell-shaped Curve - Symmetric distribution of data
Mean (μ) - Central peak and measure of central tendency
relationships: The Mean (μ) is the center point of the symmetric Bell-shaped Curve
The Standard Deviation (σ) determines the width and height of the Bell-shaped Curve; a larger σ means a wider, flatter curve
The Horizontal Axis (x) shows the range of data values distributed according to the curve's shape
The Vertical Axis (y) shows the relative likelihood (density) of each value on the Horizontal Axis (x)
